cryo-chamber.name: Cryo Chamber
cryo-chamber.description:
Cold fluorescent light stings your eyes. You're standing beside an open {cyan:cryo-pod} — yours. Frost still clings to the glass. Three other pods line the wall, dark and silent.

A {cyan:terminal} beside your pod blinks steadily. The air tastes of ozone and recycled staleness.

pod.text: Ice crystals sublimate off padding shaped to your body. The control panel reads: {cyan:SUBJECT: [REDACTED] — PRESERVATION TIME: 14,612 DAYS}. That's... 40 years.

examine.pods: Frost blankets the other three pods. Through the ice you make out shapes — but no movement, no breath fog, no signs of life. Their control panels are dead.
examine.stim: A {amber:Stim Pack} in the emergency supply bracket beside your pod. Standard cryo-recovery issue.

choices.text: Inspect the cryo-pod you woke in

corridor.name: Corridor
corridor.description: A long corridor stretches ahead, lined with {dim:pipes} and {dim:cable runs}. Emergency lighting casts everything in dim amber. One of the pipes drips something cold onto the floor in a slow, echoing rhythm.

examine.pipes: Coolant lines to the cryo-pods — most have stopped flowing. One weeps a {green:fluorescent liquid} that pools on the floor.
examine.cables: Heavy cable bundles along the ceiling. Some are severed, ends blackened. Whatever hit this place, the power grid didn't survive it.
examine.floor: Scuff marks and dust. Nobody has walked here in decades — except you, just now.
examine.light: Battery-powered emergency strips. The flicker says they won't last forever.

monitoring-station.name: Monitoring Station
monitoring-station.description:
A semicircular room crammed with monitoring equipment. Most screens are dead, but three still flicker. A faint electrical hum fills the space, raising the hairs on your arms.

A {cyan:main system terminal} scrolls cascading errors. A {green:life signs monitor} shows one active signature — yours. A {pink:radiation sensor} pulses with elevated readings.

A {amber:data chip} blinks in a reader slot.

examine.life: A single green dot — you. Heart rate slightly elevated. Neural activity off the charts. The {purple:implant} is doing something it shouldn't be.

radiation.text: The sensor maps a {pink:hot zone} in the lower levels. The tunnels below the exit hatch glow with elevated RAD readings. Survivable, but you won't want to linger.

examine.chip: A {amber:data chip} labeled {dim:PROJ. ATLAS — PHASE 3}. Whatever Phase 3 was, someone thought it worth recording.

choices.text: Pull up the system error logs

supply-closet.name: Supply Closet
supply-closet.description:
A cramped storage room, ransacked. Shelves toppled, crates pried open. The smell of old plastic and dust. Someone — or something — picked this place clean long ago.

A {amber:pipe wrench} rests on a shelf, and an {amber:intact crate} sits wedged in the corner.

examine.shelves: Labels for medical and maintenance supplies. The good stuff vanished long ago.

crate.text: You pry open the crate. Inside: two {amber:Energy Bars} in vacuum packaging. Still sealed. Still edible — probably.

examine.wrench: A heavy {amber:pipe wrench}. Solid steel. Good for turning bolts — or skulls.

exit-hatch.name: Exit Hatch
exit-hatch.description:
A heavy metal hatch set into the floor, stenciled {cyan:EMERGENCY EXIT — MAINTENANCE LEVEL}. A ladder descends into darkness. Warm air rises from below, carrying the smell of rust and damp concrete.

Your {purple:neural implant} buzzes — the signal is stronger here. Whatever is broadcasting, it's beyond these tunnels.

conditionalDesc.text:
A heavy metal hatch set into the floor, stenciled {cyan:EMERGENCY EXIT — MAINTENANCE LEVEL}. A ladder descends into darkness. Warm air rises from below, carrying the smell of rust and damp concrete.

The {pink:radiation readings} flash through your mind — the tunnels below are hot. Move quickly.

Your {purple:neural implant} buzzes — the signal is stronger here.

examine.hatch: Heavy steel with a manual release lever. The hinges groan when tested but hold. Below, metal rungs descend about 10 meters into blackness.
examine.ladder: Sturdy metal rungs. Cold to the touch. They'll hold.
